title: Batch Scheduler Status
description: Contract for <run_dir>/status.json emitted by the batch scheduler (usd-optimize-run-operations/scripts/run_batch.py). The status artifact IS the contract; CLI/dashboard views are derived from it. CRITICAL: target.state == 'done' is NOT coverage disposition 'optimized' — 'done' only proves the worker ran. The Phase-4e target_coverage.entries[]/coverage_ledger derive from target.disposition (computed from before/after deltas), never from bare 'done'. --resume re-runs only targets whose state is not terminal (done/skipped_gpu_unavailable).

cuda_available.description: CUDA signal read from the setup preflight runtime_context (never from SO hasNvidiaGpu()). When false, gpu_bound targets are skipped (state skipped_gpu_unavailable), never silently entered into a long CPU fallback.

state.description: 'timeout' is a worker killed by its per-target/per-op wall-clock budget (subprocess.run timeout) — distinct from 'failed' (worker ran to a non-zero exit or failed to spawn). Distinguishing them surfaces the per-rule-timeout hang the scheduler exists to bound.

disposition.description: Coverage disposition derived from before/after deltas, independent of state. 'done' worker with no recorded change is 'no_op'; missing summary is 'unknown' (keeps the coverage gate honest instead of fabricating 'optimized').

exit_code.description: Worker process return code; null on timeout or spawn failure.
